title: Scott's Tots

jokes.text: Andy have a boo-boo tummy
jokes.setup: Michael confronting Andy about unprofessional baby talk
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous infantile phrase for stomach pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Andy's childish nature, economical and cringey

jokes.text: Crazy diarrhea happening right now. 'Cause things can get real adult real fast.
jokes.setup: Andy defending his baby talk as preferable alternative
jokes.punchline: Graphic TMI delivered with mock-seriousness about being 'adult'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke that subverts expectations about maturity

jokes.text: Wittle-ittle, footie-wooties, num-nums, jammies, make boom-boom, widiciwus, and Whode Island
jokes.setup: Michael reading off Andy's baby talk infractions
jokes.punchline: The absurd list culminating in 'Whode Island' (Rhode Island)
jokes.explanation: Funny escalating list with great rhythm and quotable phrases

jokes.text: Do I sometimes replace Rs with Ws? Do I sometimes repeat a word to get my point across? Well, if I do, Andy's sowwy.
jokes.setup: Andy defending himself rhetorically
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves the point by saying 'sowwy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction structure - Andy proves the accusation while denying it

jokes.text: It makes me look like I hire babies
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why Andy can't use baby talk
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation that he would hire actual infants
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic - taking things to absurd literal conclusions

jokes.text: a lot of people think your Elvis voice is annoying
jokes.setup: Andy deflecting criticism
jokes.punchline: Attacking Michael's Elvis impression
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Andy's defensive nature

jokes.text: Thank you. Thank you a lot.
jokes.setup: Michael responding to compliment about his Elvis voice
jokes.punchline: Doing Elvis voice while thanking for compliment about Elvis voice
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Michael can't help himself with the Elvis voice

jokes.text: Tank you, Mr. Elvis. You're welcome, baby.
jokes.setup: Both characters embracing their criticized speech patterns
jokes.punchline: They both lean into their annoying voices simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows both characters are hopeless

jokes.text: Dr. Tuna M.D., I have some terrible news.
jokes.setup: Andy starting a medical roleplay scenario
jokes.punchline: Using 'Tuna' as doctor name (Jim's nickname) plus redundant 'M.D.'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mixing Jim's nickname with medical pretense

jokes.text: the entire office has come down with a pernicious case of the Mondays
jokes.setup: Medical diagnosis roleplay continues
jokes.punchline: Treating 'case of the Mondays' as actual medical condition
jokes.explanation: Competent but predictable joke about Monday blues

jokes.text: Don't worry. There is a surefire cure. Employee of the Month.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency requiring immediate treatment
jokes.punchline: Employee of the Month as medical cure
jokes.explanation: Good extension of medical metaphor to office solution

jokes.text: Bear Stearns, Lehman Brothers, AIG, my summer at Enron
jokes.setup: Andy listing 'awesome places' that had Employee of the Month
jokes.punchline: All companies that famously collapsed/were involved in financial scandals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Andy unknowingly lists financial disasters as success stories

jokes.text: There's about three news alerts from Nip Slip. For what? Nip Slip.
jokes.setup: Jim going through Michael's computer files
jokes.punchline: Michael's awkward denial about obvious inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael inappropriate content joke, nothing special

jokes.text: Must be hackers.
jokes.setup: Michael needing excuse for inappropriate content
jokes.punchline: Blaming hackers for porn subscriptions
jokes.explanation: Tired excuse, predictable Michael deflection

jokes.text: Did you murder someone? Worse than murder.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he did the worst thing ever
jokes.punchline: Something supposedly worse than murder (about missing Pam)
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup for Michael's typical overdramatization

jokes.text: I miss Pam.
jokes.setup: Building up to something 'worse than murder'
jokes.punchline: Just missing Pam, completely anticlimactic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff showing Michael's emotional overdramatics

jokes.text: From what? Two to four? Yep.
jokes.setup: Jim claiming he doubled his sales
jokes.punchline: From pathetically low numbers (2 to 4)
jokes.explanation: Efficient deflation of Jim's accomplishment with specific numbers

jokes.text: In approximately six hours Jim will get a phone call from David Wallace. He will be fired.
jokes.setup: Dwight in talking head making ominous prediction
jokes.punchline: Specific timeline for Jim's downfall
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dwight's scheming, ominous and specific

jokes.text: Has it really been 10 years?
jokes.setup: Michael seeing 'Scott's Tots' on his calendar
jokes.punchline: His shocked realization at the time passage
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major cringe storyline effectively

jokes.text: I have made some empty promises in my life, but hands down, that was the most generous.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on his broken promise to pay for college
jokes.punchline: Calling an empty promise 'generous' with pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - proud of his most harmful lie because of its scope

jokes.text: I bring good news, like when I promised those kids I'd pay for college.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he brings good news
jokes.punchline: Using the broken promise as example of 'good news'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Michael's continued delusion about the promise

jokes.text: I bet if you tried, you could grow the best beard of anyone in this office.
jokes.setup: Dwight approaching Jim with unexpected friendliness
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and random compliment about facial hair potential
jokes.explanation: Awkward Dwight attempt at manipulation through weird compliments

jokes.text: I didn't mean any of those things I just said.
jokes.setup: Dwight's failed attempt at flattery
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting the insincerity
jokes.explanation: Dwight's social incompetence on display

jokes.text: In an ideal world I would have all 10 fingers on my left hand, so my right hand could just be a fist for punching.
jokes.setup: Discussing ideal scenarios for Employee of the Month prize
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre anatomy preference for violence efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight logic - reorganizing human anatomy for maximum combat efficiency

jokes.text: You know what? You know, I shouldn't go in there. I'm pretty busy. I shouldn't...
jokes.setup: Michael at the school, realizing he has to face the kids
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempts to back out at the last second
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe setup, Michael's cowardice showing

jokes.text: Hey, Mr. Scott, what you gonna do / What you gonna do / Make our dreams come true
jokes.setup: Students performing elaborate thank you song for Michael
jokes.punchline: Innocent celebration of promise Michael can't fulfill
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - innocent kids celebrating Michael's lie with genuine enthusiasm

jokes.text: Jim said that?
jokes.setup: Someone telling Phyllis that Jim praised her work
jokes.punchline: Her surprised reaction suggests Jim never compliments her
jokes.explanation: Shows office dynamics and Jim's usual indifference to Phyllis

jokes.text: Number nine, number nine, Jim Halpert.
jokes.setup: Jim drawing the Employee of the Month winner
jokes.punchline: He wins his own rigged contest
jokes.explanation: Classic Office irony - well-intentioned plan backfires immediately

jokes.text: How come not...
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to process Jim winning
jokes.punchline: His brain breaking trying to form a complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his simple mind struggling with complexity

jokes.text: Yeah, but, in your perfect world, only you get money.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining there was no cash prize initially
jokes.punchline: Dwight pointing out Jim's hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight observation about Jim's convenient logic

jokes.text: All right. I came here today because I promised you tuition, and tuition is very valuable. But you know what's invaluable? Is intuition.
jokes.setup: Michael building up to tell the truth about tuition
jokes.punchline: Pivoting to wordplay between 'tuition' and 'intuition'
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael deflection using wordplay to avoid the real issue

jokes.text: I'm sorry, okay, I'm sort of spacing your name. I'm Zion. I'm Mikela's younger brother.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to personalize his apology by naming students
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't know a kid's name who then has to introduce himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's superficial relationship with the kids

jokes.text: I thought I would be by the time I was 30, but I wasn't even close, and then I thought maybe by the time I was 40, but by 40 I had less money than when I was 30.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he can't pay
jokes.punchline: His financial situation getting progressively worse with age
jokes.explanation: Painfully honest moment that's both cringe and revealing of Michael's delusions

jokes.text: Some people have evil dreams, some people have selfish dreams, or wet dreams. My dream was in the right place.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his intentions
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate 'wet dreams' reference to children
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate comment at worst possible time

jokes.text: you don't have to go to class to be in class. Online courses are a viable option
jokes.setup: Michael seeming to offer an alternative solution
jokes.punchline: Suggesting online courses as substitute for college funding
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at damage control with inadequate solution

jokes.text: which is rendered useless without batteries, and I have one for each of you.
jokes.setup: Building up to laptop gift as college substitute
jokes.punchline: Just laptop batteries, not actual laptops
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - builds up laptops then delivers just batteries

jokes.text: Hold on, hold on, hold on, hold on, hold on. They're lithium.
jokes.setup: Students' disappointed reaction to batteries
jokes.punchline: Michael emphasizing battery quality as if that helps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - doubling down on inadequate gift with technical specs

jokes.text: Son of a bitch! Pam Halpert.
jokes.setup: Drawing second Employee of the Month winner
jokes.punchline: Stanley's outburst when Pam wins
jokes.explanation: Good Stanley reaction showing the office conspiracy theories building

jokes.text: Oh, really? From what? Two to four? Yep.
jokes.setup: Questioning Pam's doubled sales claim
jokes.punchline: Same joke structure as Jim's earlier sales doubling
jokes.explanation: Direct callback to earlier joke but less effective the second time

jokes.text: Tell him I'm mad at Jim 'cause he's asking us to give money to Pam.
jokes.setup: Kevin calling David Wallace
jokes.punchline: His childish complaint about office drama
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin character moment - treating CEO like playground supervisor

jokes.text: This is Stanley Hudson. Jim Halpert is a menace.
jokes.setup: Office employees calling CEO to complain
jokes.punchline: Stanley's formal introduction and dramatic accusation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of office complaints with Stanley's dramatic language

jokes.text: They're expensive. Yeah, well, I owe you that at least, right? It's about $1,000. Really? Wow. That's over $200 a year.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to buy textbooks
jokes.punchline: His terrible math calculating cost per year
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's continued financial incompetence even in small gestures

jokes.text: No, $1,000 each year. For... Okay. Okay.
jokes.setup: Student correcting Michael's math
jokes.punchline: Michael's dawning horror at the real cost
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael realizing his offer is still too much

jokes.text: That's a lot of zeroes.
jokes.setup: Michael writing checks for textbooks
jokes.punchline: His concern about the number of zeros in $1000
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's continued anxiety about money even for smaller amounts

jokes.text: Jim, I bumped you up because I thought you could make my life easier. If you can't do that, we're gonna have a separate discussion.
jokes.setup: David Wallace getting multiple complaints about Jim
jokes.punchline: Threatening Jim's job over Employee of the Month fiasco
jokes.explanation: Fulfills Dwight's earlier prediction, showing consequences of Jim's mistake

jokes.text: I destroyed 15 young lives today. No. Yes.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on the school visit
jokes.punchline: His overdramatic assessment vs Erin's simple disagreement
jokes.explanation: Good Michael self-flagellation with Erin's simple contradiction

jokes.text: 90% of Scott's Tots are on track to graduate, and that's 35% higher than the rest of the school
jokes.setup: Michael feeling guilty about his broken promise
jokes.punchline: The promise actually motivated kids to succeed despite being fake
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - Michael's lie actually helped the kids succeed

jokes.text: Well, you know, when I hired Kevin, he was actually applying for a job in the warehouse.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about having feelings about people
jokes.punchline: Revealing Kevin was hired for wrong position entirely
jokes.explanation: Great revelation explaining Kevin's incompetence - he's in the wrong job entirely

jokes.text: What? Diabolical plan? I wouldn't even know how to begin...
jokes.setup: Ryan confronting Dwight about his plan against Jim
jokes.punchline: Dwight's terrible attempt at denial
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight bad acting when caught

tmdb_overview: Michael meets teenagers promised college tuition; Dwight sabotages employee program.
